steps.title: Identify the slope from line k
steps.content: Line $k$ is defined by $y = 6x + 4$. In slope-intercept form $y = mx + b$, the slope is $m = 6$. Since line $j$ is parallel to line $k$, and parallel lines have the same slope, line $j$ must have slope 6.

steps.title: Find the y-intercept and write the equation
steps.content: Line $j$ passes through the point $(0, 5)$. Since this point has $x = 0$, it lies on the y-axis, making it the y-intercept: $b = 5$. Using slope-intercept form with $m = 6$ and $b = 5$: $$y = 6x + 5$$ That matches choice A — A is correct.
